mechanism_narrative: A persistently high and rising unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which directly translates into reduced consumer spending and lower corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:current macro snapshot shows UNRATE at 8.5%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is above its 12-month moving average, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or near its effective lower bound, it indicates an aggressive monetary easing stance by the central bank, which eventually supports asset prices by lowering discount rates and encouraging risk-taking, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat on average (mean return not significantly positive at p<0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and a significant rise in the unemployment rate indicate a severe contraction in consumer demand and labor market health. This combination severely dampens corporate revenue and profit outlooks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:current macro snapshot shows RSAFS at 344380.0 and UNRATE at 8.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive or flat on average when both retail sales are declining by more than 1% over 3 months and unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: During a severe economic downturn, a stabilization or slight uptick in industrial production, even from low levels, signals the bottoming of the economic cycle. This improvement in supply-side activity leads to a rebound in investor sentiment and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.3755 as of t-1mo, indicating low base
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns remain negative or flat despite INDPRO showing a positive monthly change during a confirmed recessionary period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts reflects a deep contraction in the construction sector and broader economic demand. This persistent weakness signals ongoing economic headwinds that will weigh on corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are consistently positive (mean return significantly positive at p<0.05) when housing starts are below 600,000 units on a 3-month average.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable inflation rate, particularly after a period of economic stress, provides the Federal Reserve with ample flexibility to maintain accommodative monetary policy. This policy stance is generally supportive of equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat on average when the 12-month percentage change in CPIAUCSL is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job losses, as indicated by declining non-farm payrolls, directly reduce aggregate consumer spending capacity and corporate revenues. This persistent erosion of demand leads to a negative outlook for S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133019.0 as of t-1mo, indicating recent declines
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive on average (mean return significantly positive at p<0.05) during periods of sustained PAYEMS decline over a 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of contracting GDP and aggressive monetary easing (near-zero federal funds rate) indicates a severe recession where policy is attempting to stimulate growth. While policy sets the stage for future recovery, the immediate economic weakness from contracting GDP pressures corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns in the near term.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11522.1 as of t-6mo, FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently positive (mean return significantly positive at p<0.05) when GDP is contracting and FEDFUNDS is near zero.
